title: Accessible CSS
description: Focus rings, contrast, and screen-reader-only text.

options: outline: none without a replacement :focus-visible style
questions.explanation: Removing focus styles is a huge accessibility regression.

options: :focus-visible only shows when the browser thinks a visible ring is helpful (e.g. keyboard)
questions.explanation: Perfect for suppressing rings on mouse clicks but keeping them for tabbing.
display: none and visibility: hidden also hide from AT. Use the clip pattern.

questions.prompt: Reduce motion for animation-sensitive users with:
questions.explanation: A blanket rule inside prefers-reduced-motion is a common safety net.

questions.prompt: Text size that respects user preferences uses:
questions.explanation: px ignores the user's browser font-size setting.
